picks.narrative: IBP values PIT 8.7pp higher than the market — 54.6% model vs 45.2% implied. There's no standout starter, lineup, or bullpen signal driving this — the gap comes from the model's baseline read of the side, not a visible matchup edge. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.5pp): MIL defense grades +15 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +118 still sits inside the playable range (to -102). This is mostly a price play — the model likes the number more than the matchup, so the baseball case is mixed.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on WSH by 7.9pp — 44.6% fair against a 36.4% price. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-1.4pp): Miles Mikolas 4.86 xFIP vs Cam Schlittler 3.37 xFIP. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +172 still sits inside the playable range (to +146). The read here is the price, not the profile — the model just makes this a better side than the posted number does.

There's no standout starter, lineup, or bullpen signal driving this — the gap comes from the model's baseline read of the side, not a visible matchup edge. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -108 still sits inside the playable range (to -119). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +100 still sits inside the playable range (to -108). This is value in the number more than the matchup — the model just makes CHW a better side than the price does.
